phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 102.5 dBA, gradually settling to 67.5-79.5 dBA midway through session. Higher ambient noise detected; concentration requires active focus effort.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 56 bpm throughout session (unusually low resting rate, possibly indicating relaxation or fatigue). Data indicates user was in suboptimal physiological state for concentration.
phase_1.gps_motion: Geolocation data exhibits high initial uncertainty (3.9m) that improves to 2.0m as session progresses. Motion activity scattered throughout session with user frequently shifting position.

phase_2.correlations: Positional settling (GPS convergence) diverges from kinetic patterns. Acoustic fluctuation observed.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Analysis suggests reasonable concentration potential with 79.5 dB acoustic environment and mobile user behavior.

phase_3.recommendations: Use active noise cancellation or white noise to counteract loud surroundings. Address underlying restlessness through movement breaks between sessions.

reason: Score weighted toward audio environment (79.5dB—loud), variable positioning, and session duration (583s). Overall favorable conditions.
